summary: Option A implemented: JSDoc @hidden/@private/@internal detection in extract_local_declarations(). All 33 symbols removed from surface. 6 drift items across 5 pages filed as tickets in sdk-tickets-to-file.md.

changes.description: Added JSDOC_HIDDEN_RE = re.compile(r'@(?:hidden|private|internal)\b') and _has_hidden_jsdoc() helper. Updated extract_local_declarations() to call _has_hidden_jsdoc() before appending any matched declaration. Note: symbols used @private (not @hidden) — confirmed both tags cause TypeDoc to exclude.
Regenerated. namespaced_members: 1328 → 1318 (-10, the non-Amity @hidden namespaced symbols). root_exports: 124 → 99 (-25, the @hidden root exports). Amity global: 946 unchanged.

Appended section 'TS @hidden symbol cleanup (task 0083)' with 2 tickets: TICKET-0083-01 (Client.getActiveClient, 4 files) and TICKET-0083-02 (PostRepository.deletePost, 1 file). Both REWRITE category P2.

notes: Option A chosen (JSDoc scan) over Option B (hardcoded set) — more durable, catches future @hidden additions automatically. Implementation scans backwards from each declaration line through comment block lines. Regex catches @hidden, @private, and @internal per TypeDoc conventions. 6 new drift items are expected and correct: docs were referencing internal-only symbols. Tickets filed; drift left as-is per task scope. Android doc-as-test had an unrelated cache miss (JAR not found) during the CI run — not caused by this task.
